page: OVHcloud c3-256 is a compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated physical cores and 128 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz with no hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU and memory scores. It demonstrates exceptional multi-threaded performance, notably in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient for small cached blocks, though it drops to average levels for large uncached read operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform strongly, with Redis reaching top-tier throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high compute density offer strong value for CPU-bound workloads, though the 2.0 GB RAM-per-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. The server is highly suited for compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and parallel processing workloads. It is less optimal for massive in-memory databases or large-scale LLM text generation, where memory capacity or GPU acceleration would be required.
description: The OVHcloud c3-256 is a compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 128 dedicated physical cores and 128 vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core), a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 system delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, excelling in software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptography benchmarks. While its memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is outstanding, uncached large-block reads perform at average levels. This server represents a highly efficient option for compute-intensive workloads such as parallel processing, CI/CD pipelines, and high-throughput web serving, though its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it less suitable for memory-heavy databases.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-256 is a compute-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel processing.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-256 is a compute-optimized server with 128 AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps bandwidth, built for high-performance parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and NVMe storage for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
256 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
400 GB local NVMe SSD and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU, cryptography, and software compilation performance
Best suited for parallel processing, CI/CD pipelines, and web serving
